failures.label: Meet the Platform (DemoApp2 + Control Room overviews)
The Story — Before/After with chat screenshots (Demo 1)
Personas — Six customers + Gold Member Hero Banner (Demo 5)
Experiment 2 (Frequent Shopper Grid) + Experiment 3 (Family Category Boost)
Experiments 4 (Regional Price Sort) + 5 (Senior Fresh Section) with chat screenshot
failures.issues:
- **Experiment 4 caption claims "California Price Sort" but the screenshot shows a standard category grid layout (Meat & Poultry, Produce, Yogurt, etc.) — no price-sorted or regionally differentiated product listings are visible**
- **Experiment 5 has multiple chat/control room screenshots but they lack clarity on what the actual user question was and what the AI response contained — the images are too small/compressed to read the chat content with confidence**
- **The "Your Gold Member Rewards" hero banner appears in multiple Experiment 5 variations but it's unclear which variation corresponds to which targeting rule (the captions don't clearly map each screenshot to its specific boolean flag outcome)**
- **"Shop What's Fresh" section shows product images but the caption references "Emily Rodriguez (test shopper)" and a "personalized welcome message" — the personalization element is not visibly distinguishable in the screenshot**
- **Control Room screenshot at bottom is difficult to read — table text and labels are too small for a C-suite presentation**
- **The page does not clearly demonstrate the "three different outcomes for three different segments" claim in Experiment 5 — it's unclear which screenshots represent which segment/rule combination**
- **Spacing and layout feel crowded; some screenshots appear cut off or awkwardly aligned vertically**

failures.label: Demo 10 — Full A/B Lifecycle (experiment created, results, rollout, verification)

pages.label: Demo 10 — Full A/B Lifecycle (experiment created, results, rollout, verification)
Architecture + Timing table
pages.issues:
none

This is a text-only, design-focused page with no screenshots. It contains:
- Clear section headers and professional typography
- A well-structured 6-step architecture table with readable column headers and content
- A highlighted callout box explaining "Zero experiment logic in the browser" with proper visual emphasis
- Four Q&A items with icons and concise answers
- A clean demo timing schedule (0:00–20:00) with 11 clearly labeled segments
- A closing summary sentence in a green accent box with proper visual hierarchy

All text is readable, professionally formatted, and logically organized. The page serves its purpose as a reference guide for the demo structure and timing without requiring any screenshots.
